6.response: {"Q0":"No, I only picked the ones I was 100% sure were going to give me profit"}

13.response: {"Q0":"The game was really well designed. It was appealing and fun."}

0.response: {"Q0":"Well, my perception of the lottery game was negative, it seems to me that it consisted of a probability inside another probability. This among the fact that the galaxy task was a little difficult since the directions changed randomly make me realize that there was not worthy to use my cognitive resources on that task, since the whole game is really long, so I choosed not to focus on the galaxies. I feel it was the right thing to do since at the end I did not won the lottery. But I felt really focused on the digging task, that I think it mattered the most. So I'm happy."}

7.response: {"Q0":"I treated them as a gem in my mind, but I ignored them on the rain task"}

9.response: {"Q0":"Totally on visiting planets. Because it correlated the most to the earnings. I saw the galaxy task as very unlikely so a total waste in terms of cognitive resources"}

2.response: {"Q0":"It kinda seems like the odds of getting blue gems was a tiny bit larger than the red ones. But I think the reality was that both were the same."}

8.response: {"Q0":"I ignored that part the most I could. I seemed pointless to me"}

3.response: {"Q0":"Yes I did. It only happened once tho"}

12.response: {"Q0":"No. I just noticed a typo at the game over screen. In old NES fashion"}
